symptoms.symptom_name: Insidious severe dehydration and progressive confusion over days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic insidious days-long onset distinguishing HHS from the more acute DKA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known Type 2 diabetes with poor recent medication compliance
Absence of Kussmaul breathing and ketotic breath odour

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distinguishes HHS from DKA, consistent with minimal ketogenesis typical of Type 2 diabetes.
Absence of these classic DKA features argues against DKA as the primary process.
Common precipitant of HHS in elderly Type 2 diabetics.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypotension, tachycardia, severe skin tenting, dry mucous membranes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation of profound dehydration; clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Insidious severe dehydration and confusion in a known Type 2 diabetic, with a suspected precipitating UTI, together confirm evolving HHS requiring emergency transport.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate, carefully monitored IV fluid resuscitation in hospital
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive management for HHS; fluid replacement must be paced carefully to avoid rapid osmotic shifts.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum glucose, osmolality, electrolytes, and urinalysis/culture to identify the precipitating infection
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the diagnosis and identifies/guides treatment of the precipitant.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Treating only the suspected UTI at the facility without addressing the hyperglycaemic/dehydration crisis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would fail to address the immediately life-threatening metabolic derangement.
